LinCS/tiger
The System Configuration Tool

Architecture overview

This is still work in progress. To make some sense of the classes composing tiger, here's an overview of the patterns used:

  • A chain of command is implemented by classes inheriting ICommandHandle
  • In order to isolate UI-specifics from inner workings of the application classes implementing UI dialogs inherit from tiger-specific classes (containing the logic of the application), which in turn are making use of template method patterns.
  • UI classes have to mixin tiger-specific and TVision classes because of the way TVision circulates events internally.
  • A bunch of classes are singletons, the UI singleton being special in that it has to aggregate the TVision class due to the way TVision is initialized.

Dia diagrams of the classes composing tiger and their interactions:
